acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_craft_001] Pass if, by reading the revised material anchored between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:99 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, a reader can identify one contiguous scene in which Gavin makes a wrong call, corrects it from a visible feature of the notation, lands on a provisional translation, and reacts physically or behaviorally before the prose returns to summary. Fail if the breakthrough is still reported mainly as retrospective 'he decoded' narration.

[chapter_04_award_001] Pass if, before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:131, the revised chapter contains at least two concrete beats where regime pressure or social fallout interrupts Gavin's work and changes his choices, and if that line of moral cost carries directly into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:187. Fail if the chapter still reads as three mostly separate blocks: Babbling scene, research montage, Duncan coda.

[chapter_04_prose_001] Pass if, across the revised explanatory passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:115, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:181, no more than two negation-led recalibration constructions remain and every new decoded term is introduced by a visible mark, spatial relation, or bodily sensation before any abstract paraphrase. Fail if the passage still depends on repeated 'not X' framing to do most of the explanatory work.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by reading the revised material anchored between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:99 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, a reader can identify one contiguous scene in which Gavin makes a wrong call, corrects it from a visible feature of the notation, lands on a provisional translation, and reacts physically or behaviorally before the prose returns to summary. Fail if the breakthrough is still reported mainly as retrospective 'he decoded' narration.
findings.problem: The chapter's key craft obligation, Gavin's first genuine decoding breakthrough, is delivered mostly as retrospective report. The prose tells us that weeks pass, invariants emerge, and concepts resolve, but it rarely stays inside one live attempt long enough for the breakthrough to feel earned in scene. For a foundational turn in the book's logic, the chapter summarizes process where it needs staged error, pressure, and consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the decoding stretch beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:89 and climaxing around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:99, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:105, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117 into one anchored decoding night. Keep the Wexworth setup, then dramatize Gavin testing a wrong hypothesis, noticing a specific positional invariant, revising the reading, and paying a physical or academic cost in the moment. Compress surrounding montage material instead of summarizing the breakthrough itself.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:131, the revised chapter contains at least two concrete beats where regime pressure or social fallout interrupts Gavin's work and changes his choices, and if that line of moral cost carries directly into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:187. Fail if the chapter still reads as three mostly separate blocks: Babbling scene, research montage, Duncan coda.
findings.problem: The chapter's announced dual pressure, the manuscript's intellectual difficulty and the Carrow regime's worsening human damage, is structurally split rather than fully braided. After the Babbling scene, the chapter spends most of its middle movement inside manuscript work and only restores Duncan's suffering at the end, so the human cost reads as a coda instead of a constant contaminant. That keeps the chapter below shortlist-level integration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread occupied-school pressure through the middle section at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:97, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:99, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:113, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:119 so the decoding advances in the presence of other people's damage rather than apart from it. Options are flexible, but at least one breakthrough should be purchased by a missed human response or an interruption tied to the regime before the dinner scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:131.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised explanatory passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:115, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:181, no more than two negation-led recalibration constructions remain and every new decoded term is introduced by a visible mark, spatial relation, or bodily sensation before any abstract paraphrase. Fail if the passage still depends on repeated 'not X' framing to do most of the explanatory work.
findings.problem: The prose leans too often on corrective-negation and abstract restatement when introducing the First Art. Repeated moves like defining terms by what they are not, then paraphrasing them at conceptual altitude, start to read as a house tic instead of discovery. That flattens the uncanny texture and drifts away from the style-bible demand for concrete, perceptual specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:109, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:115, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:181, replace most of the negation-led glosses with direct positive description from Gavin's sensory frame: the spacing, hooks, depth effect, drift, bodily reaction, and the exact visual relation that makes him risk each gloss. Keep at most the strongest one or two corrective lines.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
